name: Brown Chicken Stock- Master Chefs
recipeIngredient: 5 lb Chicken, parts (backs, necks, carcasses, and giblets), no livers
2 medium Carrots, peeled, trimmed, coarsely chopped
2 large Celery stalks with leaves, trimmed, coarsely chopped
2 each Thyme sprigs OR 1 pinch Dried Thyme
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat your oven to 450°F.
2. Roast the chicken bones in a roasting pan in the oven for 30 minutes.
3. Add the vegetables and roast for an additional 15 minutes.
4. Transfer everything to a stockpot.
5. Deglaze the roasting pan with a little water, scraping up the browned bits, and add them to the stockpot.
6. Add cold water to cover by about 2 inches and bring to a boil, skimming all of the froth from the surface as it forms.
7. Lower the heat and add all of the remaining ingredients except the peppercorns. Simmer uncovered for 3 hours, adding water as needed to cover the ingredients and skim when necessary.
8. Add peppercorns for the last fifteen minutes of the simmering process.
9. Strain the stock into a large bowl through a colander lined with a double layer of dampened cheesecloth. Gently press the solids to extract all of the liquid possible.
10. Discard the solids and cool the liquid to room temperature.
11. Refrigerate until chilled; lift off the solid fat that forms at the surface. Discard the fats.
12. Pour the stock into containers for storage, label and date.
13. Stock keeps for about 3 days in the refrigerator and up to six months in the freezer.
